name: Unknown Rules
short_description: Unknown Rules is an independently created multiplayer online horror game that supports 1-4 players.
detailed_description:
Note: Text translation within the game has not been implemented yet. If you have requirements for text translation, please wait for the official version!

Game Introduction

Unknown Rules¬†is an independently developed first-person horror puzzle game.

‚Äč‚ÄčFrontroom (Pre-Backroom) Introduction‚Äč‚Äč

Every region has its "chosen unlucky ones"‚ÄĒordinary people who, through sheer coincidence, fall into the Backroom. Before their descent, however, they were simply diligent workers in their respective Frontrooms. You can choose to work alone or team up with friends to complete your tasks. Perhaps, during your routine, an unexpected journey awaits.

*****************************************************************************
Note: Only one Frontroom level can be selected. All Frontroom levels lead to Backroom Level 0.

‚Äč‚ÄčLevel 1: Office‚Äč‚Äč
You are an ordinary office worker. On this day, the office is eerily empty. After working overtime, you decide to rest your head on the desk for a nap. When you awaken, the environment has shifted into something unrecognizable.

‚Äč‚ÄčLevel 2: Logistics Warehouse‚Äč‚Äč
You are a night-shift warehouse packer. As usual, you retrieve, package, and ship orders. But upon completing your quota, you discover an unsettling note.

‚Äč‚ÄčBackroom Introduction‚Äč

‚ÄčCongratulations‚ÄĒyou‚Äôve been chosen. Due to the scale of certain levels, please review the level descriptions before proceeding.

*****************************************************************************
‚Äč‚ÄčCurrently Developed Backroom Levels‚Äč‚Äč
(Descriptive reference order only; not actual gameplay sequence)
Note: Backroom levels are unlocked progressively and cannot be freely selected.

  • ‚Äč‚ÄčLevel 0: The Darkroom‚Äč‚Äč

Procedurally generated terrain using PCG technology. Requires real-time lighting (RTX 2060 or higher recommended). A fixed-map version for lower-end GPUs will be added later.

  • ‚Äč‚ÄčLevel 37: Sublimity‚Äč‚Äč

A serene "poolcore" environment optimized for multiplayer fun. Currently uses Lumen soft ray tracing.

  • ‚Äč‚ÄčLevel 9: Suburbs‚Äč‚Äč

A windswept, sinister suburban nightmare.

  • ‚Äč‚ÄčLevel 1: Abandoned Public Zone‚Äč

‚Äč

  • ‚Äč‚ÄčLevel 6: Lights Off‚Äč‚Äč

Technical note: Current scanning relies on ray tracing (RTX 2060+ required). A low-end GPU alternative will use basic particle scanning (less precise, ignores moving objects, no object data‚ÄĒscenery only).

Some ‚Äč‚ÄčKey Items‚Äč‚Äč

To survive, manage and use every item you find. Your inventory includes ‚Äč‚Äč8 backpack slots‚Äč‚Äč and ‚Äč‚Äč2 pocket slots‚Äč‚Äč.

  • ‚Äč‚ÄčEnergy Drink‚Äč‚Äč: Temporarily boosts stamina.

  • ‚Äč‚ÄčSanity Pills‚Äč‚Äč: Stabilizes your mental state.

  • ‚Äč‚ÄčBatteries‚Äč‚Äč: Restores power to electronic devices.

  • ‚Äč‚ÄčMysterious Notes‚Äč‚Äč: Provide clues to solve puzzles.

‚Äč‚ÄčCore Mechanics‚Äč‚Äč

  • ‚Äč‚ÄčStamina‚Äč‚Äč: Limited endurance‚ÄĒcritical during escapes.

  • ‚Äč‚ÄčSanity‚Äč‚Äč: Fear gnaws at your mind when encountering the supernatural.

  • ‚Äč‚ÄčHealth‚Äč‚Äč: Survivable injuries unless facing overwhelmingly powerful entities.

‚Äč‚ÄčParty Mode‚Äč‚Äč

Boredom is inevitable, so I‚Äôm developing open-ended features to keep players engaged. Two experimental party maps are now available:

  • ‚Äč‚ÄčLevel 1: Go Parlor‚Äč

For Go enthusiasts: Challenge a friend to a match while listening to music. Note: This mode is unpolished due to limited development time, but it supports full Go matches.

  • ‚Äč‚ÄčLevel 2: Cinema‚Äč‚Äč

Input a video URL (.MP4, .m3u8, or untested rtmp/rtsp streams) to watch together. Basic playback is functional, though features are incomplete. Contact the developer for issues.

‚Äč‚ÄčIn Development‚Äč‚Äč

  • ‚Äč‚ÄčNew Backroom Level‚Äč‚Äč: Level 94 "Animation"

  • ‚Äč‚ÄčNew Frontroom Level‚Äč‚Äč: Amusement Park
